title: Never silent-green — capture coarse bug on unrecognized test output
context: The failure-learning loop must capture bugs from test failures in various output formats (TAP, spec reporter, etc.). The system must decide what to do when a test run fails but the output format isn't recognized by the parser.
decision: If a test run exits nonzero but output doesn't match recognized formats (TAP or spec), capture a single coarse bug from the last 40 lines of raw output rather than silently reporting success.
consequences: Unrecognized test failures are never lost (safety over precision)
Coarse bugs have lower signal (full cmdStr + raw tail) than parsed TAP/spec failures, reducing synthesizer actionability
Downstream bug records are less precise: `{ test: cmdStr, message: 'Test run failed (exit N); output not TAP.\n<tail>' }` vs parsed failure names
Operators always see that the suite failed, even if parsing breaks
alternatives_rejected: Silently succeed when output unrecognized — loses the failure entirely, making the worst failure mode invisible
Fail hard and exit on parse failure — prevents any bug capture and breaks the learning loop
Retry with multiple parser strategies — adds complexity for rare edge cases
